summary: The heap dump analysis identifies a significant memory leak caused by a java.lang.Object[] instance retained by the main thread. This object occupies 94.84% of the heap, likely due to unbounded growth in an ArrayList.

top_retained_objects.agent_note: This object array is the primary suspect, retaining the majority of the heap.
The ArrayList retains a large portion of the heap, likely due to unbounded growth.

dominant_allocator_stacks.leak_pattern: The ArrayList grows unbounded due to improper handling of its size.

root_cause.description: Unbounded growth of an ArrayList in the main thread.
root_cause.detailed_explanation: The ArrayList in the main thread is growing without bounds, likely due to repeated additions without proper size management. This results in a large Object[] being retained in memory, consuming 94.84% of the heap. The issue originates from the org.test.HeapDumper.main method.

remediation: Review the org.test.HeapDumper.main method and identify where elements are being added to the ArrayList.
Implement proper size management for the ArrayList, such as limiting its growth or clearing it when no longer needed.
Consider using a data structure with bounded capacity if unbounded growth is not required.
Add logging or monitoring to track the size of the ArrayList during runtime.
Refactor the code to ensure that objects are not unnecessarily retained in memory.
